title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins anticipate Hagens may turn pro soon and boost their lineup for the regular season's final stretch.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney indicated discussions will consider Hagens' integration into the team this spring.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' is a WWII-themed follow-up film streaming on Netflix featuring Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan.
'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett as a comedian processing his divorce, is streaming on Disney+.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber about professional bowling's unique characters, is available on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora, in his eighth season as Red Sox manager, is unsure about his future beyond his contract ending in 2027.
Cora's priority in future decisions includes his family's needs, particularly his children's education.
Cora is 134 wins away from surpassing Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
Zacha leads with a career-best 23 goals and 51 points in the current 66-game stretch.
Boston’s second line has the second-best NHL goal differential, trailing only Colorado’s top line.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric cancer care at MGH.
Inspired by young patients and previous experience, this is Mahr's second time running with Mass General Hospital assistance.
Mahr highlights the need for funding pediatric cancer research, stating it's a responsibility to support affected families.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts income tax proposal suggests reducing the rate from 5% to 4%, potentially appearing on the November ballot.
Tufts University analysis estimates the tax cut could reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually, impacting state-funded programs.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing concerns over economic competitiveness and essential state services funding.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in Salisbury.
Prosecutors highlight evidence including a voodoo doll and Church's statements about poisoning Fowler if he cheated.
Defense argues Church filmed Fowler to address his substance abuse and claims the evidence against her is circumstantial.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and allowing crossbow use for all hunters.
MassWildlife reports deer populations exceed goals, with significant overpopulation in central, western, and eastern Massachusetts.
The proposed changes include reducing bowhunting setbacks and expanding access to more than 40,000 acres of state-owned land.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny of Cape Cod Magnet Crew found a derringer pistol in a river in Bridgewater.
The pistol was in a pristine condition despite being in a submerged case for decades, likely due to its stainless steel construction.
DeMontigny's find, shared on his YouTube channel, quickly gained online traction with over 1.2 million views on Reddit.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, faces murder charges after a Chelsea stabbing left one dead.
Chelsea police found multiple stabbing victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue around 6:45 p.m. Thursday.
A 25-year-old man died after multiple stab wounds; other victims' identities remain undisclosed.
